side pocket.quote: I love me some side pocket shots.

...into the side are wide and you can send it right to the middle of the pocket and have some room for error.
    
      The same wide-cut openings make the bank look like it's going into a 6-inch wide side pocket, even though it isn't, so the bank looks friend-shaped.

foosball.quote: Darts, shuffleboard, foosball, golf, bowling. My dad always said his putting helped his pool/snooker game, and vice versa.
Darts, shuffleboard, foosball, golf, bowling. My dad always said his putting helped his pool/snooker game, and vice versa.

A lot of people who aren't doing this for a living take it way too seriously and forget it's just a bar game. If I see a foosball table, I'm not looking up the international tournament rules, I'm just playing the way we did in school.

...had a basement table, the basement was big enough for a sectional and "movie theater" with a pull down screen, projector and surround sound, beside an office on the back half of the garage, a bar and foosball table on either end of the pool table.

Mentally tough, never gives up, great all round game, a player you have to beat, he is the T-800 of the snooker world, remorseless. The guy has been though a lot, his Mum abandoned him when he was 8 and his Dad died when Selby was 16. The guy is a legend and a good shout for 5th greatest player of all time.
Get used to it. In-game ads are the future. It’s the Americanisation of all sport.
Get used to it. In-game ads are the future. It’s the Americanisation of all sport.
It's 20 years since gambling ads were made legal and I still find it weird when I see them. Imo gambling ads should be banned across the board.

Was about to say that. Not just snooker but gambling ads and sports go hand in hand. Just like the ban on tobacco previously it would make sense to remove that link, though not sure if that actually reduced the number of smokers, I'm sure someone will post...

IOC recognition of cue sports and Olympic status are separate things. Funding also differs greatly from country to country, with some countries providing little or no support outside Olympic sports, while others provide support for IOC-recognized non-Olympic sports as well.
    
      In Canada, we barely provide meaningful support for most sports outside the major Olympi...
I'm not so bothered about those, as they don't interrupt the frames, and I just mute the ads.
I'm not so bothered about those, as they don't interrupt the frames, and I just mute the ads.

If they can ban advertising for fatty or sugary foods. It’s terrible the amount of constant gambling ads on tv, and radio. I’m constantly having to explain to my kids it’s not a fun game ap that’s been advertised it’s trying to take your money and you won’t win against them in the long run.

...ages gone in 2 minutes. Sitting on your phone in bed, watching tv etc, and gone in a moment. Really should be banned from advertising in sport. A few sports podcasts I listen to don't do any gambling ads and have cut out anyone talking about betting. While only small, at least it doesn't normalise it any more.
Channel 5 has ruined snooker for me...it's all about the ads.
